subject: Anthropology
title: Sociolinguistics
description: A quick, practical guide to Sociolinguistics.

hook.text: You speak differently to your boss than to your best friend. Your language shifts to match the social moment.

buildup.text: Sociolinguistics studies how language varies across social groups and situations — class, gender, age, and region.

discovery.text: William Labov showed that even small pronunciation differences signal social class in places like New York City.

twist.text: There's no correct dialect — only prestigious ones. Standard language is a political choice, not a linguistic one.

climax.text: How you speak reveals where you're from, who you know, and where you sit in the social hierarchy.

punchline.text: Every accent tells a social story.

quiz.question: What did Labov's research demonstrate?
options: All people speak identically in cities
